Atelectasis.title: Atelectasis overview
Atelectasis.snippet: Atelectasis appears as volume loss with increased density and possible fissure displacement.

Cardiomegaly.title: Cardiothoracic ratio
Cardiomegaly.snippet: Cardiomegaly is suggested when the cardiothoracic ratio exceeds approximately 0.5 on a PA chest radiograph.

Effusion.title: Pleural effusion findings
Effusion.snippet: Pleural effusion may cause blunting of the costophrenic angle and meniscus sign.

Infiltration.title: Interstitial and alveolar infiltrates
Infiltration.snippet: Infiltrates can represent inflammatory, infectious, or edema-related opacities in the lung fields.

Mass.title: Pulmonary mass assessment
Mass.snippet: A pulmonary mass is typically defined as an opacity greater than 3 cm and requires further characterization.

Nodule.title: Pulmonary nodule basics
Nodule.snippet: Pulmonary nodules are focal round opacities up to 3 cm; risk stratification depends on size and patient factors.

Pneumonia.title: Pneumonia radiographic pattern
Pneumonia.snippet: Lobar or patchy consolidation with air bronchograms may support a diagnosis of pneumonia.

Pneumothorax.title: Pneumothorax signs
Pneumothorax.snippet: A visible pleural line with absent peripheral lung markings suggests pneumothorax.

Consolidation.title: Consolidation features
Consolidation.snippet: Consolidation is homogeneous increased pulmonary parenchymal attenuation obscuring vascular margins.

Edema.title: Pulmonary edema clues
Edema.snippet: Cardiogenic pulmonary edema may show bilateral perihilar opacities, vascular congestion, and pleural effusions.

Emphysema.title: Emphysema imaging
Emphysema.snippet: Hyperinflation, increased lucency, and flattened diaphragms are common chest X-ray findings in emphysema.

Fibrosis.title: Pulmonary fibrosis features
Fibrosis.snippet: Reticular opacities, architectural distortion, and reduced lung volumes can indicate fibrotic lung disease.

Pleural_thickening.title: Pleural thickening
Pleural_thickening.snippet: Pleural thickening appears as focal or diffuse pleural opacity and may reflect prior inflammation or exposure.

Hernia.title: Hiatal hernia on chest X-ray
Hernia.snippet: A retrocardiac air-fluid level may suggest a hiatal hernia on frontal chest radiographs.

No finding.title: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality
No finding.snippet: No dominant acute radiographic abnormality was identified in the current image.
